description_long: This bungalow features a steeply pitched gable roof clad in reddish-brown tiles, extending over a rectangular structure with beige horizontal siding and a stone foundation. The front facade includes a small covered porch with wooden railings and steps, flanked by a large window and a main entrance door. The side elevations reveal a combination of single and double windows, with one side showing a secondary entrance to the porch. The rear of the bungalow is plain, with no windows or doors, emphasizing privacy. The building sits on a patch of green grass, suggesting a suburban or residential neighborhood setting. The overall design is simple yet functional, with clear residential intent and modest decorative elements.
description: A beige, gable-roofed bungalow with a covered front porch, stone foundation, and multiple windows, set on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, steps, and a large window beside the door.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the steep gable end, two rectangular windows, and a secondary porch entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain wall, no windows or doors, and the full expanse of the roof visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view featuring a gable end, a large double window, and no porch access.
